name: Mont-ros and Baumes de Caixurma Trail
name_original: Baumes de Caxurma desde Castellfollit de la Roca - trail run circular por el Parque Natural de la zona volcánica de la Garrotxa
description: This difficult 6.6-mile trail run from Castellfollit de la Roca explores the Baumes de Caxurma in the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Castellfollit De La Roca offer a diverse landscape shaped by ancient lava flows and river erosion. The region is characterized by its distinctive basaltic cliff, which the town perches upon, and is part of the La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park. Runners can explore trails winding through volcanic cones, lush valleys, and extensive forests of beech, oak, and pine. The terrain generally features gentle gradients, making it accessible for various fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Castellfollit De La Roca The most popular jogging route is Mont-ros and Baumes de Caixurma Trail, a 6.6 miles (10.6 km) difficult trail that takes 1 hour 29 minutes to complete, exploring parts of the volcanic zone. Another top favourite among local runners is Virgin of the Devesa – Baumes de Caixurma loop from Castellfollit de la Roca, a difficult 10.6 miles (17.1 km) path offering extensive views of the volcanic landscape. Local runners also love the Cingles de Frontfreda loop from Sant Joan les Fonts, a 3.9 miles (6.3 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 40 minutes. Jogging around Castellfollit De La Roca is defined by volcanic landscapes, basaltic cliffs, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging routes with significant elevation gain. The routes in Castellfollit De La Roca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2400 runners have used komoot to explore Castellfollit De La Roca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: There are nearly 200 running routes around Castellfollit De La Roca, offering a wide variety of options for different fitness levels. These include 5 easy routes, over 100 moderate routes, and more than 80 challenging trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in this region?
A: The jogging trails in Castellfollit De La Roca feature a diverse landscape shaped by ancient lava flows and river erosion. You'll find paths winding through the &lt;strong&gt;La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;, with its volcanic cones and lava flows, as well as lush valleys and extensive forests of beech, oak, and pine. The terrain generally offers gentle gradients, but more challenging routes with significant elevation gain are also available.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running routes in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: Yes, the region offers several routes with gentle gradients suitable for families. While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly listed, the 5 easy routes and many of the 105 moderate routes would be good starting points. Look for trails that stick to the flatter river valleys or less steep forest paths for a pleasant family outing.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: Absolutely. Many of the running routes in the area are loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20089294" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castellfollit de la Roca loop from Castellfollit de la Roca&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 5.4 km path offering great views of the town and its surroundings.

Q: What are some notable natural features or landmarks I might see while running?
A: The most iconic feature is the dramatic &lt;strong&gt;basaltic cliff&lt;/strong&gt; upon which Castellfollit de la Roca is perched. You can also explore the unique volcanic landscape of the &lt;strong&gt;La Garrotxa Volcanic Zone Natural Park&lt;/strong&gt;. Along some routes, you might encounter impressive waterfalls such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/262644" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riera de Sant Aniol Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3304941" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Can Cotilla Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baumes de Caixurma&lt;/a&gt; caves are also a point of interest on some trails.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: The running routes in Castellfollit De La Roca are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 20 reviews. More than 2,400 runners have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the unique volcanic landscapes and the scenic beauty of the basaltic cliff and river valleys.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced trail runners?
A: Yes, the region offers plenty of challenging options. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14972090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Turó de Sant Valentí loop from Sant Joan les Fonts&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 12.3 km route with significant elevation gain, perfect for those seeking a more strenuous run through the volcanic landscape.

Q: Are there any running routes that explore the volcanic zone?
A: Many routes traverse the volcanic landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e957183208/mont-ros-and-baumes-de-caixurma-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mont-ros and Baumes de Caixurma Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 10.6 km route that takes you directly through parts of the volcanic zone, offering a chance to run amidst ancient geological formations and explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2830180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Baumes de Caixurma&lt;/a&gt; caves.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Castellfollit De La Roca?
A: The region's extensive forests of beech, oak, and pine provide shaded paths, making it pleasant for running during warmer months. However, the mild climate of Catalonia generally allows for enjoyable running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer particularly comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural scenery.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of the iconic basaltic cliff?
A: Yes, several routes provide stunning views of the basaltic cliff and the town perched atop it. Jogging along the riverbanks of the Fluvià and Toronell rivers, which flank the cliff, offers unique perspectives. The Josep Pla viewpoint, located at the end of the cliff, also provides panoramic vistas of the surrounding valleys, which can be incorporated into or viewed from nearby running paths.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by waterfalls?
A: Yes, the area is rich with natural beauty, including several waterfalls. While not every running route passes directly by them, you can find trails that lead to or near attractions like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5532262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Waterfall and Natural Pool of the Fluvià River&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6153432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorg de Can Batlle&lt;/a&gt;, offering a refreshing stop during your run.
tips: Beautiful bridge with a view of the castle. Worth a visit.

The Baumes de Caixurma are a complex system of labyrinthine galleries and narrow cracks that run between large blocks.
Located south of Castellfollit de la Roca, within the municipality of Montagut and Oix.

These are blocks of bluish-gray shale marl. According to the cartography of the Geological Institute of Catalonia, they are Eocene materials from the Lutetian.

The entrances to the baumes, with an approximate length of 400m, are hidden by the dense vegetation

Sanctuary of the Virgen de la Devesa is a building in the municipality of Montagut i Oix (Garrotxa) included in the Inventory of the Architectural Heritage of Catalonia.

It is a small building of Romanesque origin (first documented in 1261), with a rectangular floor plan and a single nave. Its apse is semicircular and the entrance door has an archivolt in relief. It has been reformed on many occasions, the most important of which was in 1540.

Runes of the church of the Virgin of Mont-ros located at the top of Mont-ros, in the northwest buttress of the Sant Julià del Mont mountain range. The first writings in which we find this chapel documented are from 1240.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this trail run?
faqs.answer: The Komoot tour data suggests an average duration of approximately 1 hour and 30 minutes for jogging. However, due to the varied and challenging terrain, including sections requiring scrambling, many trail runners might find it takes closer to 2-3 hours, especially if taking time to explore the Baumes de Caixurma or enjoy the views.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Baumes de Caxurma trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Castellfollit de la Roca. You can usually find parking available within the village, which is known for its dramatic location. It's advisable to arrive early, especially on weekends or during peak season, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail run?
faqs.answer: The terrain is highly varied and challenging. You'll encounter a mix of accessible paths, narrow sections, and forest trails, including holm oak forests. Expect roots, small stream crossings, and sections under road bridges. Closer to the Baumes de Caixurma, the path becomes more technical with 'labyrinthine galleries and narrow cracks' that require scrambling over large blocks. Proper trail running shoes with good grip are essential.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or children?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as 'difficult' for a trail run and is generally considered a 'moderate hike'. It is not recommended for beginners due to significant elevation changes, technical sections, and areas requiring scrambling. It is also not suitable for wheelchairs and not recommended for young children due to the uneven terrain and narrow cracks.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: Key highlights include the dramatic village of Castellfollit de la Roca itself, perched on a basaltic cliff, and the unique geological formations of the Baumes de Caixurma. You'll also pass by the Virgin of the Devesa and the Ruins of the Church of Mare de Déu de Mont-ros, offering historical and scenic stops. Don't miss the View of Castellfollit de la Roca from the footbridge near the start.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Natural Park of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone?
faqs.answer: No, there are typically no entrance fees or permits required to access the general trails within the Natural Park of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone, including this route. However, always respect local regulations and signage, especially concerning protected areas like the Baumes de Caxurma.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to run this trail?
faqs.answer: Autumn is particularly noted for its beauty in the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone, with vibrant fall colors. Spring also offers pleasant temperatures and lush greenery. Summer can be hot, so an early morning start is recommended. Winters are generally mild but check weather conditions for potential rain or colder temperatures.

faqs.question: Is the Baumes de Caxurma trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally allowed on trails in the Natural Park of the Garrotxa Volcanic Zone, the challenging terrain of this specific route, with narrow cracks and scrambling sections, might be difficult or unsafe for some dogs. If you bring your dog, ensure they are agile, well-behaved, and kept on a leash, especially near protected wildlife areas like the Baumes de Caxurma.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route partially follows sections of other trails. You will encounter parts of the Volta a peu a la Garrotxa, Montagut-Ermita de la Devesa-Baumes de la Caxurma, Via Verda del Fluvià, and smaller sections of trails known as '13', 'la Passarel·la', and 'Pont Medieval'.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this trail run?
faqs.answer: Given the challenging and varied terrain, it's crucial to bring appropriate trail running shoes with good grip. Other essentials include sufficient water, snacks, a fully charged phone, a basic first-aid kit, and layers of clothing suitable for changing weather conditions. A headlamp might be useful if you anticipate being out near dusk or want to explore the Baumes de Caxurma more closely.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or facilities near the trailhead in Castellfollit de la Roca?
faqs.answer: Yes, Castellfollit de la Roca is a village with amenities. You can find cafes, restaurants, and other facilities in the village before or after your run. It's a good place to refuel and enjoy the unique atmosphere.
